golden_rules: NEVER ask for one answer — always ask for 3-5 options
Temperature 0.85 is the sweet spot for ideation
Temperature 0.3 for evaluation/convergence (unusual for this model)
Temperature 1.0 for naming and wild brainstorming
Use as FIRST model in chain, not last
Excels at divergent thinking, struggles with convergent
10 calls at $0.0003 each = $0.003 for breadth that would cost $0.03 with expensive models

chain_pattern.stage2_evaluate: DeepSeek-chat or Hermes-3-405B — pick the best
chain_pattern.stage3_refine: Qwen3.5-397B or Seed-2.0-pro — polish the winner

prompt_patterns.brainstorm: Generate {N} diverse approaches to {PROBLEM}. Each must be meaningfully different. For each: name, description, one strength, one risk.
prompt_patterns.diverge_converge: First, brainstorm 7 wild ideas for {PROBLEM}. Then, evaluate each against these criteria: {CRITERIA}. Rank them and explain your top 2 choices.
prompt_patterns.naming: Generate {N} creative names for {THING}. For each, explain the etymology and why it fits. Be playful and unexpected.
prompt_patterns.what_if: What if {VARIABLE} changed for {SITUATION}? Explore 3 possible outcomes, each more surprising than the last.
prompt_patterns.perspective: Analyze {PROBLEM} from the perspective of {STAKEHOLDER}. What do they care about? What would they propose? What would they object to?

seed_2_0_mini.cost_efficiency: 10x cheaper than Hermes-3-405B for comparable brainstorming quality. 30x cheaper than Qwen3.5-397B. The most cost-effective creative model on DeepInfra.

temperature_guide.0.3: Convergence — picking the best from a list, evaluation mode
temperature_guide.0.5: Focused creativity — when you need some structure
temperature_guide.0.7: Balanced — good for reverse engineering, analysis
temperature_guide.0.85: Sweet spot — default for all ideation tasks
temperature_guide.0.9: High divergence — diverge_converge stage 1, wild ideas
temperature_guide.1.0: Maximum creativity — naming, wild brainstorming, breaking constraints
